groups.label: Setup
groups.description: Open the canonical scene, target the canonical avatar, and prepare the default ASM-Lite prefab scaffold in one operator-controlled setup suite.

suites.label: Setup Scene / Avatar / Prefab
suites.description: Open the canonical scene, open ASM-Lite, select Oct25_Dress, add the default prefab scaffold, and confirm the rebuild action is available when the operator runs this setup suite.
suites.expectedOutcome: The canonical scene is loaded, ASM-Lite targets Oct25_Dress, the prefab scaffold is present, and the rebuild action is visible without running lifecycle steps.
suites.debugHint: Run this combined setup suite before lifecycle or runtime suites when Unity is not already on the canonical scene/avatar/prefab context.

cases.label: Scene, avatar, and prefab setup
cases.description: Establish the canonical scene, editor avatar context, and prefab scaffold only when the operator runs this suite.
cases.expectedOutcome: Assets/Click ME.unity is loaded, ASM-Lite is attached to Oct25_Dress, and the primary rebuild action is visible.
cases.debugHint: If setup stalls, verify the scene and avatar names match the catalog fixture, inspect prefab prerequisites, and confirm the ASM-Lite window is not hidden behind another dock tab.

steps.label: Open Click ME scene
steps.description: Load the canonical smoke-test scene on operator request.
steps.expectedOutcome: Assets/Click ME.unity is the active Unity scene.
steps.debugHint: If the scene does not load, confirm the fixture scene exists at Assets/Click ME.unity.

steps.label: Open ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the product window through the automation entrypoint.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window instance is available and focused for automation.
steps.debugHint: If no window appears, inspect editor layout state and window initialization logs.

steps.label: Select Oct25_Dress
steps.description: Bind the canonical avatar fixture to the automation window.
steps.expectedOutcome: Oct25_Dress is the active automation target in the ASM-Lite window.
steps.debugHint: If selection fails, confirm the avatar exists in Assets/Click ME.unity and is not hidden or renamed.

steps.label: Add ASM-Lite prefab
steps.description: Add the ASM-Lite prefab scaffold to the current automation target.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite prefab scaffold is attached to Oct25_Dress and generation-ready.
steps.debugHint: If add-prefab fails, inspect avatar prerequisites and generated asset path state before retrying.

steps.label: Verify rebuild action is visible
steps.description: Assert that the primary rebuild action is now available in the window.
steps.expectedOutcome: The primary rebuild action is visible, signaling that the scaffold install succeeded.
steps.debugHint: If the primary action does not change, inspect whether the prefab install completed and whether the window refreshed.

suites.label: Setup Package Presence
suites.description: Confirm the ASM-Lite package, menu/window entrypoint, prefab source, catalog, and host readiness before deeper setup suites run.
suites.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite package surface is present, the automation window opens, canonical catalog loading works, and the smoke host is ready.
suites.debugHint: If this suite fails, confirm the package is installed in the external UAT project and the canonical package prefab/catalog paths resolve.

cases.label: Window menu opens
cases.description: Open the ASM-Lite editor window through the automation entrypoint.
cases.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite editor window is available for automation.
cases.debugHint: If opening fails, inspect menu registration and editor window initialization.

steps.label: Open ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the product window through the automation entrypoint.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is available and focused for automation.
steps.debugHint: If no window appears, inspect editor layout state and window initialization logs.

cases.label: Package resources present
cases.description: Confirm package-managed resources required by setup automation are available.
cases.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite prefab source resolves from the package.
cases.debugHint: If the prefab is missing, inspect package installation and embedded package paths.

steps.label: Verify package prefab source
steps.description: Assert that the package-managed ASM-Lite prefab source resolves.
steps.debugHint: If the asset does not resolve, confirm the package path and prefab import state.

cases.label: Canonical catalog loads
cases.description: Confirm the smoke host can load the canonical suite catalog.
cases.expectedOutcome: The canonical smoke suite catalog loads with required metadata.
cases.debugHint: If catalog loading fails, inspect Tools/ci/smoke/suite-catalog.json schema and JSON validity.

steps.label: Load canonical smoke catalog
steps.description: Load the canonical smoke catalog through the test-side catalog reader.
steps.expectedOutcome: The canonical smoke suite catalog loads successfully.
steps.debugHint: If loading fails, inspect catalog schema validation diagnostics.

cases.label: Host ready
cases.description: Confirm the smoke host can report baseline readiness.
cases.expectedOutcome: The host readiness check passes.
cases.debugHint: If readiness fails, inspect host startup state and command directory setup.

steps.label: Verify host readiness
steps.description: Assert that the smoke host is ready to process setup actions.
steps.debugHint: If readiness fails, inspect host startup state and session paths.

suites.label: Setup Scene Acquisition
suites.description: Validate canonical scene acquisition from common editor states and expected diagnostics for invalid scene paths.
suites.expectedOutcome: The canonical scene opens from supported editor states and invalid scene inputs report deterministic diagnostics.
suites.debugHint: If scene acquisition fails, verify Assets/Click ME.unity exists in the external UAT project and inspect scene path overrides.

cases.label: Canonical scene already open
cases.description: Open the canonical scene when it is already the target scene.
cases.expectedOutcome: The canonical scene remains active and setup can continue.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect active-scene path normalization.

steps.label: Open canonical scene
steps.description: Open Assets/Click ME.unity.
steps.debugHint: If the scene does not load, confirm the fixture scene exists.

cases.label: Different scene open
cases.description: Open the canonical scene when another scene is active.
cases.expectedOutcome: The host switches to Assets/Click ME.unity.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect scene manager load diagnostics.

steps.label: Switch to canonical scene
steps.description: Create a temporary different scene state, then open Assets/Click ME.unity.
steps.debugHint: If switching fails, confirm the temporary scene mutation ran and no unsaved prompt blocked scene load.

cases.label: Temp unsaved scene open
cases.description: Create a temporary unsaved setup scene state, then reacquire the canonical scene.
cases.expectedOutcome: The host returns to Assets/Click ME.unity from a temporary scene state.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect fixture mutation cleanup and scene restore diagnostics.

steps.label: Open canonical scene after temp scene
steps.description: Apply the temporary scene setup mutation and open Assets/Click ME.unity.
steps.expectedOutcome: Assets/Click ME.unity is active after temporary scene setup.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect temp scene mutation cleanup.

cases.label: Missing scene diagnostic
cases.description: Try to open a missing scene path as an expected failure.
cases.expectedOutcome: The step reports SETUP_SCENE_MISSING without aborting the expected-failure case.
cases.debugHint: If the diagnostic changes, inspect scene path validation before OpenScene.

steps.label: Open missing scene
steps.description: Attempt to open a scene path that is intentionally absent.
steps.debugHint: If this unexpectedly succeeds, verify the missing test path is still absent.

cases.label: Non-scene path diagnostic
cases.description: Try to open a non-scene package asset path as an expected failure.
cases.expectedOutcome: The step reports SETUP_SCENE_PATH_INVALID without aborting the expected-failure case.
cases.debugHint: If the diagnostic changes, inspect path extension validation before scene loading.

steps.label: Open non-scene path
steps.description: Attempt to open a package JSON path as a scene.
steps.debugHint: If this unexpectedly succeeds, verify non-scene path validation runs first.

suites.label: Setup Window Launch and Focus
suites.description: Validate ASM-Lite editor window launch, reuse, focus, and recovery after a closed automation window.
suites.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window opens from closed state, reuses/focuses when already open, and reopens cleanly after a close.
suites.debugHint: If focus is unreliable, inspect Unity editor layout state and window lifecycle logs before treating it as a product setup failure.

cases.label: Closed to open
cases.description: Close any existing automation window, then open and focus ASM-Lite.
cases.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window opens and is focused.
cases.debugHint: If opening fails, inspect editor window initialization logs.

steps.label: Close existing window
steps.description: Close any existing ASM-Lite automation window.
steps.expectedOutcome: No existing ASM-Lite automation window remains open.
steps.debugHint: If close fails, inspect Unity editor window lifecycle.

steps.label: Open ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the ASM-Lite automation window.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is available and focused.
steps.debugHint: If open fails, inspect menu/window initialization.

steps.label: Verify focused window
steps.description: Assert the ASM-Lite automation window has editor focus.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is the focused editor window.
steps.debugHint: If focus fails, inspect dock/tab state and retry after closing stale windows.

cases.label: Already open reuse focus
cases.description: Open ASM-Lite twice and confirm the existing automation window remains focused.
cases.expectedOutcome: The existing window is reused and focused.
cases.debugHint: If a duplicate or unfocused window appears, inspect OpenForAutomation behavior.

steps.label: Open ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the ASM-Lite automation window.
steps.debugHint: If open fails, inspect window initialization.

steps.label: Reuse ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the ASM-Lite automation window again.
steps.expectedOutcome: The existing ASM-Lite window is reused and focused.
steps.debugHint: If reuse fails, inspect GetWindow behavior.

steps.label: Verify focused window
steps.description: Assert the ASM-Lite automation window has editor focus.
steps.debugHint: If focus fails, inspect Unity layout state.

cases.label: Stale closed window handled
cases.description: Close the automation window and reopen it cleanly.
cases.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window reopens and focuses after a stale/closed state.
cases.debugHint: If reopen fails, inspect stale editor window references.

steps.label: Open ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the ASM-Lite automation window.
steps.debugHint: If open fails, inspect window initialization.

steps.label: Close ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Close any open ASM-Lite automation window.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite automation window is closed.
steps.debugHint: If close fails, inspect stale editor window references.

steps.label: Reopen ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Reopen the ASM-Lite automation window after closing it.
steps.debugHint: If reopen fails, inspect stale editor window references.

steps.label: Verify focused window
steps.description: Assert the reopened ASM-Lite automation window has editor focus.
steps.expectedOutcome: The reopened ASM-Lite window is focused.
steps.debugHint: If focus fails, inspect Unity layout state.

suites.label: Setup Avatar Discovery and Selection
suites.description: Validate deterministic avatar discovery, selected-object precedence, ambiguity handling, inactive avatar policy, prefab asset rejection, and missing-avatar diagnostics.
suites.expectedOutcome: Avatar selection resolves only deterministic scene avatar targets and reports coded diagnostics for invalid or ambiguous targets.
suites.debugHint: If these cases fail, inspect Unity selection state, fixture mutation cleanup, prefab asset persistence, and avatar-name normalization before deeper setup suites.

cases.label: Selected canonical avatar
cases.description: Select the canonical scene avatar before running avatar selection.
cases.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar is used as the automation target.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-scene-avatar precedence.

steps.label: Selected canonical avatar
steps.description: Select the canonical scene avatar before running avatar selection.
steps.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar is used as the automation target.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-scene-avatar precedence.

cases.label: Find by fixture name
cases.description: Clear Unity selection and resolve the canonical avatar by configured fixture name.
cases.expectedOutcome: The active scene avatar matching the fixture name is selected.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect avatar name normalization and active scene object discovery.

steps.label: Find by fixture name
steps.description: Clear Unity selection and resolve the canonical avatar by configured fixture name.
steps.expectedOutcome: The active scene avatar matching the fixture name is selected.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect avatar name normalization and active scene object discovery.

cases.label: Wrong object selected
cases.description: Select a non-avatar scene object that shares the fixture target name.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SELECTED_OBJECT_NOT_AVATAR is reported without mutating a prefab asset.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected object type checks before name lookup.

steps.label: Wrong object selected
steps.description: Select a non-avatar scene object that shares the fixture target name.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SELECTED_OBJECT_NOT_AVATAR is reported without mutating a prefab asset.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected object type checks before name lookup.

args.expectedDiagnosticContains: selected object is not a valid avatar

cases.label: Wrong avatar selected
cases.description: Select an alternate scene avatar while the fixture name points at the canonical avatar.
cases.expectedOutcome: The selected avatar wins over fixture-name lookup deterministically.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence and wrong-avatar fixture setup.

steps.label: Wrong avatar selected
steps.description: Select an alternate scene avatar while the fixture name points at the canonical avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: The selected avatar wins over fixture-name lookup deterministically.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence and wrong-avatar fixture setup.

cases.label: Selected inactive avatar
cases.description: Select the canonical avatar while it is inactive.
cases.expectedOutcome: The explicitly selected inactive scene avatar is accepted.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-object policy versus active-name lookup policy.

steps.label: Selected inactive avatar
steps.description: Select the canonical avatar while it is inactive.
steps.expectedOutcome: The explicitly selected inactive scene avatar is accepted.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-object policy versus active-name lookup policy.

cases.label: Unselected inactive avatar
cases.description: Make the canonical avatar inactive and clear Unity selection.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because inactive unselected avatars are not surprise-selected.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect activeInHierarchy filtering in avatar name lookup.

steps.label: Unselected inactive avatar
steps.description: Make the canonical avatar inactive and clear Unity selection.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because inactive unselected avatars are not surprise-selected.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect activeInHierarchy filtering in avatar name lookup.

cases.label: Duplicate avatar name
cases.description: Create a duplicate active scene avatar with the fixture name and clear selection.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_AMBIGUOUS is reported until the operator selects a target.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect duplicate-name counting and selection cleanup.

steps.label: Duplicate avatar name
steps.description: Create a duplicate active scene avatar with the fixture name and clear selection.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_AMBIGUOUS is reported until the operator selects a target.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect duplicate-name counting and selection cleanup.

cases.label: Selected duplicate avatar disambiguates
cases.description: Create a duplicate active scene avatar and select the canonical avatar.
cases.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar disambiguates duplicate names.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence over ambiguous name lookup.

steps.label: Selected duplicate avatar disambiguates
steps.description: Create a duplicate active scene avatar and select the canonical avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: The selected canonical avatar disambiguates duplicate names.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selected-avatar precedence over ambiguous name lookup.

cases.label: Prefab asset avatar selected
cases.description: Select a prefab asset containing an avatar descriptor.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_PREFAB_ASSET is reported because prefab assets are not scene avatar instances.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect EditorUtility.IsPersistent checks before scene object selection.

steps.label: Prefab asset avatar selected
steps.description: Select a prefab asset containing an avatar descriptor.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_PREFAB_ASSET is reported because prefab assets are not scene avatar instances.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect EditorUtility.IsPersistent checks before scene object selection.

cases.label: Missing avatar
cases.description: Clear selection and override the fixture name to an intentionally missing avatar.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported for the missing fixture avatar.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selection cleanup and avatarName overrides.

steps.label: Missing avatar
steps.description: Clear selection and override the fixture name to an intentionally missing avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported for the missing fixture avatar.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect selection cleanup and avatarName overrides.

cases.label: Same-name non-avatar ignored
cases.description: Create a same-name scene object without VRCAvatarDescriptor while targeting that name.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because non-avatar objects are ignored during name lookup.
cases.debugHint: If this fails, inspect descriptor filtering during name lookup.

steps.label: Same-name non-avatar ignored
steps.description: Create a same-name scene object without VRCAvatarDescriptor while targeting that name.
steps.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported because non-avatar objects are ignored during name lookup.
steps.debugHint: If this fails, inspect descriptor filtering during name lookup.

suites.label: Setup Scaffold Add and Idempotency
suites.description: Validate no-component scaffold add flow, repeated add safety, existing component primary action, and selection-change resilience.
suites.expectedOutcome: Scaffold add and existing component flows are stable and idempotent.
suites.debugHint: Inspect fixture mutation details, action hierarchy assertions, and prefab add steps.

cases.label: No component exposes add prefab primary
cases.description: No component exposes add prefab primary
cases.expectedOutcome: No component exposes add prefab primary
cases.debugHint: Inspect Phase 06 scaffold/idempotency evidence.

suites.label: Generated Asset Readiness
suites.description: Validates setup coverage for generated ASM-Lite asset readiness, missing/stale generated folders, and package-managed references.
suites.expectedOutcome: Generated readiness cases confirm setup add/rebuild affordances and package-managed generated references without destructive drills.
suites.debugHint: Inspect generated folder state, fixture mutation cleanup, and action hierarchy output.

cases.label: Clean add creates generation-ready scaffold
cases.description: Remove any existing ASM-Lite component, add the prefab scaffold, and confirm setup can reach a generation-ready state.
cases.expectedOutcome: Add Prefab attaches the ASM-Lite scaffold from a clean avatar baseline.
cases.debugHint: Inspect remove-component fixture setup and add-prefab automation output.

steps.description: Apply missing-generated-folder and assert Rebuild is the primary action.
steps.debugHint: Inspect generated folder mutation cleanup and action hierarchy output.

cases.label: Stale generated folder handled
cases.description: Apply a stale generated folder and assert the setup flow recognizes Rebuild as the recovery action.
cases.expectedOutcome: Rebuild is recognized as primary action when stale generated assets are present.
cases.debugHint: Inspect stale generated folder fixture state and action hierarchy output.

steps.label: Open Scene
steps.description: Open canonical smoke scene.
steps.debugHint: Inspect scene path.

steps.label: Open Window
steps.description: Open ASM-Lite window.
steps.debugHint: Inspect window state.

steps.label: Select Avatar
steps.description: Select Oct25_Dress.
steps.debugHint: Inspect avatar lookup.

steps.label: Assert Rebuild for stale generated folder
steps.description: Apply stale-generated-folder and assert Rebuild is the primary action.
steps.debugHint: Inspect stale generated folder cleanup and action hierarchy output.

cases.label: Generated references package-managed by default
cases.description: Add the prefab scaffold from a clean baseline and assert generated references remain package-managed by default.
cases.expectedOutcome: Generated references stay package-managed after the default setup add flow.
cases.debugHint: Inspect ASMLiteComponent generated asset ownership fields and generated reference paths.

steps.label: Open Scene
steps.description: Open canonical smoke scene.
steps.debugHint: Inspect scene path.

steps.label: Open Window
steps.description: Open ASM-Lite window.
steps.debugHint: Inspect window state.

steps.label: Select Avatar
steps.description: Select Oct25_Dress.
steps.debugHint: Inspect avatar lookup.

steps.label: Add Prefab before package-managed assertion
steps.description: Apply remove-component and add the ASM-Lite prefab scaffold.
steps.expectedOutcome: Prefab scaffold is attached before generated reference assertion.
steps.debugHint: Inspect component attach state before asserting generated references.

steps.label: Assert package-managed generated references
steps.description: Assert generated references are package-managed by default after Add Prefab.
steps.expectedOutcome: Generated references are package-managed by default.
steps.debugHint: Inspect ASMLiteComponent generated asset ownership fields and generated reference paths.

suites.label: Setup Negative Diagnostics
suites.description: Validate safe setup diagnostic paths for bad scene, package resource, and avatar targets using expected-failure semantics.
suites.expectedOutcome: Each safe negative reports the locked diagnostic code and useful human text without destructive project mutation.
suites.debugHint: If a negative diagnostic drifts, inspect the owning setup action and expectedDiagnostic args before changing product behavior.

cases.label: Missing scene diagnostic
cases.description: Try to open a scene path that is intentionally absent.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SCENE_MISSING is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: If this unexpectedly succeeds, verify the missing test path is still absent.

steps.label: Assert missing scene diagnostic
steps.description: Attempt to open Assets/Missing.unity and require the missing-scene diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect scene path validation before OpenScene.

cases.label: Non-scene path diagnostic
cases.description: Try to open an existing package JSON path as a scene.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SCENE_PATH_INVALID is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: If the diagnostic changes, inspect path extension validation before scene loading.

steps.label: Assert non-scene path diagnostic
steps.description: Attempt to open package.json as a Unity scene and require the invalid-path diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect path extension validation before scene loading.

cases.label: Missing package resource diagnostic
cases.description: Check an intentionally absent package prefab path without mutating package installation.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_PACKAGE_RESOURCE_MISSING is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: If this unexpectedly succeeds, verify the missing package resource path is still absent.

steps.label: Assert missing package resource diagnostic
steps.description: Attempt to resolve a missing package prefab source and require the resource diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect package resource path override handling.

cases.label: Missing avatar diagnostic
cases.description: Use a fixture avatar-name override that does not match any descriptor-bearing scene avatar.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_NOT_FOUND is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: Inspect avatar name lookup and descriptor filtering.

steps.label: Assert missing avatar diagnostic
steps.description: Apply missing-avatar fixture state and require the not-found diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect avatar lookup and fixture override name.

cases.label: Duplicate avatar ambiguity diagnostic
cases.description: Create duplicate descriptor-bearing avatars with the fixture target name and no disambiguating selection.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_AMBIGUOUS is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: Inspect duplicate-name fixture cleanup and selected-avatar disambiguation policy.

steps.label: Assert duplicate avatar ambiguity diagnostic
steps.description: Apply duplicate-avatar fixture state and require the ambiguous diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect duplicate descriptor-bearing scene avatar setup.

cases.label: Prefab asset avatar selected diagnostic
cases.description: Select a prefab asset instead of a scene avatar instance.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_AVATAR_PREFAB_ASSET is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: Inspect prefab asset selection checks before scene-avatar lookup.

steps.label: Assert prefab asset diagnostic
steps.description: Apply selected-prefab-asset fixture state and require the prefab-asset diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect prefab asset type checks.

cases.label: Wrong object selected diagnostic
cases.description: Select a non-avatar scene object and require the selected-object diagnostic.
cases.expectedOutcome: SETUP_SELECTED_OBJECT_NOT_AVATAR is reported as an expected failure.
cases.debugHint: Inspect selected object type checks before name lookup.

steps.label: Assert wrong object selected diagnostic
steps.description: Apply wrong-object-selection fixture state and require the selected-object diagnostic.
steps.debugHint: Inspect selected-object type checks and fixture cleanup.

args.expectedDiagnosticContains: selected object is not a valid avatar

groups.label: Lifecycle
groups.description: Exercise the package-managed lifecycle actions that rewire or detach ASM-Lite content.

suites.label: Rebuild / Vendorize / Detach / Return
suites.description: Run the deterministic lifecycle roundtrip from rebuild through package-managed return.
suites.expectedOutcome: Lifecycle actions complete in order and the avatar returns to a clean package-managed ASM-Lite state.
suites.debugHint: If the roundtrip drifts, inspect generated asset ownership and whether the avatar still points at vendorized content.

cases.label: Package-managed roundtrip
cases.description: Confirm lifecycle actions can mutate state and return to the package-managed baseline.
cases.expectedOutcome: The lifecycle roundtrip completes without leaving the avatar in a detached or stale vendorized state.
cases.debugHint: Use generated asset paths and install-path diagnostics to isolate which lifecycle transition drifted.

steps.label: Rebuild ASM-Lite assets
steps.description: Rebuild generated ASM-Lite assets for the canonical avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: Generated assets rebuild successfully and reflect the latest package-managed source.
steps.debugHint: If rebuild fails, inspect generated asset write locations and the selected avatar binding.

steps.label: Hygiene cleanup after rebuild
steps.description: Reset known ASM-Lite lifecycle drift before the next lifecycle action.
steps.expectedOutcome: Known ASM-Lite generated/vendorized state is returned to a package-managed baseline.
steps.debugHint: If hygiene cleanup fails, inspect package-managed restore and known generated asset paths before continuing.

steps.label: Vendorize generated assets
steps.description: Copy generated assets under Assets/ASM-Lite for vendorized workflow coverage.
steps.expectedOutcome: Vendorized generated assets exist and the avatar now points at the vendorized copy.
steps.debugHint: If vendorize fails, inspect whether the generated asset target path is writable and deterministic.

steps.label: Hygiene cleanup after vendorize
steps.description: Return known ASM-Lite vendorized state to package-managed baseline before detach coverage.
steps.expectedOutcome: Vendorized references and known generated state are cleaned back to package-managed baseline.
steps.debugHint: If hygiene cleanup fails, preserve the state for review and inspect stale vendorized references.

steps.label: Detach ASM-Lite component
steps.description: Detach the editable ASM-Lite component while preserving the baked content.
steps.expectedOutcome: The avatar is detached from the editable component without breaking the generated state.
steps.debugHint: If detach leaves a broken avatar, inspect component cleanup and generated controller references.

steps.label: Hygiene cleanup after detach
steps.description: Restore the detached avatar to package-managed baseline before final return verification.
steps.expectedOutcome: Detached state is recovered and known ASM-Lite generated state is package-managed again.
steps.debugHint: If hygiene cleanup fails, preserve the state for review and inspect detached recovery diagnostics.

steps.label: Return to package-managed state
steps.description: Reattach the avatar to package-managed ASM-Lite assets and clear vendorized drift.
steps.expectedOutcome: The avatar returns to a package-managed ASM-Lite state with no lingering vendorized dependency.
steps.debugHint: If return-to-package-managed fails, inspect whether detach or vendorize left stale asset references behind.

groups.label: Playmode / Runtime
groups.description: Validate the runtime-facing ASM-Lite path inside a playmode transition without restarting the host session.

suites.label: Enter / Validate / Exit Playmode
suites.description: Prepare the canonical avatar, enter playmode, validate runtime state, and return to edit mode.
suites.expectedOutcome: The canonical avatar enters playmode with valid runtime ASM-Lite state and exits back to edit mode cleanly.
suites.debugHint: If runtime validation fails, inspect whether the prefab scaffold was present before entering playmode and whether playmode exited cleanly.

cases.label: Runtime validation
cases.description: Prepare the avatar for runtime checks and verify the runtime component stays valid across the playmode boundary.
cases.expectedOutcome: ASM-Lite playmode preprocessing completes and returns to edit mode without requiring a new Unity session.
cases.debugHint: If playmode hangs or exits dirty, inspect editor scaffold setup, VRCFury/NDMF preprocessing, and playmode state transitions before rerunning.

steps.label: Open ASM-Lite window
steps.description: Open the product window before runtime setup begins.
steps.expectedOutcome: The ASM-Lite window is available for runtime preparation.
steps.debugHint: If the window is missing, inspect editor window lifecycle before entering playmode.

steps.label: Select Oct25_Dress
steps.description: Target the canonical avatar fixture before runtime preparation.
steps.expectedOutcome: Oct25_Dress is the active runtime-preparation target.
steps.debugHint: If selection fails, confirm the canonical avatar still exists in the loaded scene.

steps.label: Ensure ASM-Lite scaffold exists
steps.description: Add the ASM-Lite prefab scaffold if the avatar is not already prepared for runtime checks.
steps.expectedOutcome: The avatar has the ASM-Lite scaffold required for runtime validation.
steps.debugHint: If scaffold setup fails, inspect editor-side generation prerequisites before entering playmode.

steps.label: Enter playmode
steps.description: Transition the Unity host into playmode for runtime coverage.
steps.expectedOutcome: The host enters playmode and begins runtime validation on the canonical avatar.
steps.debugHint: If enter-playmode hangs, inspect unsaved scene prompts or compilation state before retrying.

steps.label: Validate runtime component
steps.description: Assert that ASM-Lite survives playmode preprocessing on the prepared avatar.
steps.expectedOutcome: The prepared avatar is valid after playmode preprocessing; editor-only ASM-Lite component stripping is accepted.
steps.debugHint: If runtime validation fails, inspect generated parameter/controller state, VRCFury/NDMF preprocessing, and avatar lookup diagnostics.

steps.label: Exit playmode
steps.description: Return the host to edit mode after runtime validation completes.
steps.expectedOutcome: The host exits playmode cleanly and returns to edit mode without restarting Unity.
steps.debugHint: If exit-playmode fails, inspect playmode teardown logs and pending scene state.
